title: aponogetonaceae
intro: The Aponogetonaceae rank, also known as the Aponogetonaceae family, refers to a group of monocotyledonous flowering plants commonly known as the water widgeon family. This family consists of about 25 species of aquatic plants, often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Aponogetonaceae plants are typically characterized by their long, narrow leaves and small, white or yellow flowers that bloom above water. They are often grown in aquatic gardens for their unique and attractive foliage.
